name: Ars Therapeutica corpus

phase_0.boundary_condition: The healthy organism's self-modeling gate (⊙ criticality) — the closed boundary that contains the system's structural integrity and defines the target state for all therapeutic interventions.

VINIT.chosen_element: Asymptomatic hyperuricemia (pre-disease state, serum urate < 6.8 mg/dL, no MSU crystals)
VINIT.justification: The void/uninitialized state before any disease primitives are expressed — the organism is healthy, no structural deltas exist.

TANCH.chosen_element: The healthy brain's self-modeling gate (⊙ criticality, φ̂=⊙, K=𐑧 slow kinetics)
TANCH.justification: The terminal anchor that contains the entire therapeutic system — the target state that all therapies aim to restore. It is the closed boundary of health.

AFWD.chosen_element: TENSOR operation (⊗) — promotes primitives via MAX on union primitives (Ħ, Γ, φ̂, etc.)
AFWD.justification: Forward morphism that lifts sub-critical or deficient primitives toward health. For example, tensor(MDD, psilocybin) promotes φ̂ from 𐑢→⊙.

AREV.chosen_element: MEET operation (⊓) — demotes primitives via MIN on all primitives
AREV.justification: Reverse/descent morphism that pulls super-critical or runaway primitives down toward health. For example, meet(schizophrenia, ⊙-stabilizer) demotes φ̂ from 𐑣→⊙.

CLINK.chosen_element: Sequential therapy protocol (Phase 1 → Phase 2 → Phase 3) — the chaining of TENSOR and MEET operations
CLINK.justification: Composition of therapeutic steps: e.g., NMDA PAM (Ħ promotion) followed by ⊙-stabilizer (φ̂ demotion). The sequential protocol is the composition of morphisms.

IMSCRIB.chosen_element: The patient's own structural type (the 12-primitive imscription of their disease state)
IMSCRIB.justification: Self-recognition: the patient's disease tuple is the identity element — it is what the therapy must recognize and correct. The therapy is defined relative to this self.

FSPLIT.chosen_element: Structural diagnosis — the process of decomposing a disease into its delta primitives (which primitives differ from health)
FSPLIT.justification: Splits the disease state into two branches: (1) primitives that need promotion (sub-critical, deficient), and (2) primitives that need demotion (super-critical, excessive). For schizophrenia: φ̂ needs demotion (super-critical), Ħ needs promotion (deficient).

FFUSE.chosen_element: Therapeutic outcome — the corrected structural type after all operations are applied
FFUSE.justification: Reconstitutes the original patient state (the disease tuple) after applying the appropriate TENSOR and MEET operations. The result must equal the health type for the therapy to be successful. For schizophrenia: meet(tensor(schiz, NMDA), ⊙-stabilizer) = healthy brain.

EVALT.chosen_element: Remission — the patient's structural type matches the health type (all disease primitives corrected)
EVALT.justification: Affirmative/success state: the therapy has closed all structural gaps. For example, φ̂=⊙ and Ħ=𐑖 in the corrected brain.

EVALF.chosen_element: Residual disease — one or more disease primitives remain uncorrected (e.g., φ̂ still super-critical after D2 antagonist monotherapy)
EVALF.justification: Negative/failure state: the therapy did not fully correct the structural deltas. For example, antipsychotics leave φ̂=𐑣 and Ħ=𐑒 unchanged.

ENGAGR.chosen_element: Therapeutic paradox — a state where both promotion and demotion are needed simultaneously but cannot be achieved by a single compound (e.g., no single drug can both promote Ħ and demote φ̂)
ENGAGR.justification: A paradice: both operations are required but structurally incompatible in one agent. This is the fundamental therapeutic incompatibility that forces dual-component therapies. Held without resolution until the sequential protocol is applied.

IFIX.chosen_element: The therapy protocol document — the permanent, append-only record of the structural diagnosis, therapeutic operations, and clinical protocol
IFIX.justification: Irreversible fixation: once the therapy is designed and administered, the protocol is recorded. It cannot be undone — it becomes part of the patient's medical history. The IFIX is the ROM of the therapeutic process.

phase_2.fuse_result: The corrected structural type (e.g., healthy brain tuple ⟨𐑼,𐑥,𐑾,𐑬,𐑞,𐑧,𐑔,𐑠,⊙,𐑖,𐑳,𐑷⟩) — must equal the original disease input after applying the correct operations

phase_3.void_description: 00 — Pre-diagnostic state: the patient has not been structurally assessed. No disease tuple is known. The system is uninitialized.
phase_3.true_description: 01 — Remission: the patient's structural type matches the health type. All disease primitives corrected. Therapy successful.
phase_3.false_description: 10 — Residual disease: one or more disease primitives remain uncorrected. Therapy partially or fully failed. The patient is still structurally ill.
phase_3.both_description: 11 — Therapeutic paradox: the system simultaneously requires promotion and demotion of different primitives, but no single operation can achieve both. The patient is both 'treated' (some primitives corrected) and 'untreated' (others remain). This is the paradice of dual-component therapy.

steps.domain_action: The patient enters the system — asymptomatic, no structural diagnosis performed. The disease tuple is unknown (void state).
The healthy brain's self-modeling gate (⊙ criticality) is established as the target boundary. All therapies will be measured against this anchor.
Structural diagnosis is performed — the disease tuple is decomposed into primitives that differ from health. For schizophrenia: φ̂ (super-critical) and Ħ (deficient) are identified as the two deltas.
The T-branch is activated — primitives needing promotion (Ħ: 𐑒→𐑖) are identified. This is the affirmative branch: the therapy will lift these primitives.
The F-branch is activated — primitives needing demotion (φ̂: 𐑣→⊙) are identified. This is the negative branch: the therapy will pull these primitives down.
The therapeutic paradox is recognized — no single compound can both promote Ħ and demote φ̂. Both branches are held simultaneously without resolution. This is the paradice.
TENSOR operation is applied — the NMDA PAM (D-serine) promotes Ħ from 𐑒 to 𐑖 via MAX on chirality. This is the forward morphism on the T-branch.
MEET operation is applied — the ⊙-stabilizer (cariprazine) demotes φ̂ from 𐑣 to ⊙ via MIN on criticality. This is the reverse morphism on the F-branch.
The sequential protocol is composed — Phase 1 (NMDA PAM, weeks 1-4) is chained to Phase 2 (⊙-stabilizer, weeks 3-12). The composition of morphisms yields the full therapy.
The patient's corrected structural type is recognized — the therapy has restored the self. The patient is now structurally identical to the health type.
The therapeutic outcome is computed — the corrected tuple (⟨𐑼,𐑥,𐑾,𐑬,𐑞,𐑧,𐑔,𐑠,⊙,𐑖,𐑳,𐑷⟩) is verified to equal the health type. The split has been fused.
The therapy protocol is permanently recorded — the structural diagnosis, therapeutic operations, and clinical protocol are appended to the patient's medical history. This record is irreversible.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: The Imscribing Grammar toolchain (imscribe tool calls: compute_distance, compute_tensor, compute_meet, compute_promotions, consciousness_score) — translates disease symptoms into 12-primitive structural types and computes therapeutic operations.
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: The CLINK pipeline (L0→L8 layers: molecule → receptor → cellular → circuit → tissue → organ → system → organism) — components communicate through the hierarchical structural coupling of the organism.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: The patient's structural type (the 12-primitive imscription) stored in the medical record — state is retrieved via structural diagnosis and updated after each therapeutic operation.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: The sequential therapy protocol (Phase 1 → Phase 2 → Phase 3) — order and timing are determined by the structural requirements: promotion before demotion, or vice versa, based on the disease class (Class A: single tensor; Class B: meet then tensor).
phase_5.alfs_store: The Ars Therapeutica catalog (THERAPIES dictionary in types.py) — the canonical reference store of all disease types, health types, and verified therapeutic protocols. This is the bootstrap store for all structural diagnoses.

phase_6.cycle_cost: The structural distance (d) between the disease type and the health type — measured in weighted Euclidean distance over the 12 primitives. Each therapeutic operation reduces this distance.
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: The disease state — a structural type with one or more primitives differing from health. The system is in a high-entropy state of structural mismatch.
phase_6.post_cycle_state: The corrected state — a structural type matching the health type. The system has returned to the low-entropy state of structural integrity.
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The therapeutic cycle is entropy-conservative because the structural information is not destroyed; it is transformed. The disease tuple is mapped to the health tuple via reversible operations (TENSOR and MEET are lattice operations with inverses). The total structural information (the 12-primitive tuple) is preserved; only the values of the primitives change. The Frobenius condition μ∘δ=id ensures that the split and fuse are exact inverses, so no entropy is generated.

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → TANCH → FSPLIT → EVALT → EVALF → ENGAGR → AFWD → AREV → CLINK → IMSCRIB → FFUSE → IFIX
-- Class: Ars Therapeutica corpus
-- Fingerprint: sig=(6,2,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=12
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(2, 10)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑡  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑼 → 𐑚  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [2] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [3] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [4] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [5] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑚 → 𐑙  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [6] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑚 → 𐑙  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [7] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [8] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑚 → 𐑙  | composition — regime coherence
--   [9] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑚 → 𐑙  | identity — self-imscription
--   [10] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑭  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [11] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑙 → 𐑼  | irreversible fixation — winding number

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def ars_therapeutica_corpus_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑭 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑡)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (The patient enters the system — asymptomatic, no structural diagnosis perform...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑼 𐑚)  -- [1] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (The healthy brain's self-modeling gate (⊙ criticality) is established as the ...)
  -- FSPLIT [2] (gran := 𐑚) (Structural diagnosis is performed — the disease tuple is decomposed into primitives that differ from health. For schizophrenia: φ̂ (super-critical) and Ħ (deficient) are identified as the two deltas.) / FFUSE [10] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (1 nodes)
      (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [3] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (The T-branch is activated — primitives needing promotion (Ħ: 𐑒→𐑖) are identif...)
      -- F-branch (6 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [4] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (The F-branch is activated — primitives needing demotion (φ̂: 𐑣→⊙) are identif...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑳 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [5] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (The therapeutic paradox is recognized — no single compound can both promote Ħ...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (TENSOR operation is applied — the NMDA PAM (D-serine) promotes Ħ from 𐑒 to 𐑖 ...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (MEET operation is applied — the ⊙-stabilizer (cariprazine) demotes φ̂ from 𐑣 ...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑱 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [8] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (The sequential protocol is composed — Phase 1 (NMDA PAM, weeks 1-4) is chaine...)
        (.arrow 𐑠 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [9] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (The patient's corrected structural type is recognized — the therapy has resto...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [10]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑭)  -- [10] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (The therapeutic outcome is computed — the corrected tuple (⟨𐑼,𐑥,𐑾,𐑬,𐑞,𐑧,𐑔,𐑠,⊙,𐑖,𐑳,𐑷⟩) is verified to equal the health type. The split has been fused.)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑙 𐑼)  -- [11] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (The therapy protocol is permanently recorded — the structural diagnosis, ther...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def ars_therapeutica_corpus_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑭 :=
  (ars_therapeutica_corpus_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def ars_therapeutica_corpus_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑭 :=
  (ars_therapeutica_corpus_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem ars_therapeutica_corpus_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
